title: Nemcia crenulata
intro: Nemcia crenulata, also known as the 'Fern-leaved Pea', is a perennial legume native to Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the relief of pain and the protection against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Nemcia crenulata include isoflavones, such as genistein and daidzein, as well as alkaloids and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Nemcia crenulata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to support menopausal health, bone health, and cardiovascular well-being.
taxonomy: Nemcia crenulata belongs to the family Fabaceae. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Nemcia, which is endemic to Australia. Nemcia crenulata is a legume native to Western Australia. The plant is a shrub with small, white flowers and is part of the larger group of pea flowers. It is a relatively rare species within the genus Nemcia.
